title: Predicted Occurrence
description: 4.0.0. A first-class record of an EXPECTATION: an occurrent type predicted, by a stated predictor, to occur over a stated window. A prediction is NOT an assertion that the thing happened - it is the sibling of the token_occurrence that has not (yet) happened, and it satisfies no rule that requires a token_occurrence (Rule 24). Its interval MUST carry exactly ONE temporal dimension: a wall-clock start (with optional end) or an ordinal start_tick (with optional end_tick), never both and never neither. The optional strength is the predicted probability - part of the PREDICTED CONTENT itself, identity-bearing when present, not a provenance evaluation (the narrow, deliberate 4.0.0 extension of Principle P4).

instantiates.description: the occurrent TYPE predicted to occur; MANDATORY (P1)

predictor.description: the predicting source - a MODELED agent or construct inside the ontology (a continuant type or a particular token individual), never a cryptographic key

strength.description: OPTIONAL, identity-bearing when present: the predicted probability, part of the predicted content itself, NOT a provenance evaluation (4.0.0 Locked Decision extension of Principle P4)

start.description: RFC 3339, UTC, mandatory Z suffix - the wall-clock dimension

start_tick.description: the ordinal dimension: a discrete tick with NO wall-clock mapping (Rule 23)

predicted_interval.description: the predicted window. Rule 24 enforces exactly ONE temporal dimension: start (wall-clock, optional end) or start_tick (ordinal, optional end_tick), never both (dimension_conflict, HARD) and never neither (missing_dimension, HARD)
